claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child you're claiming for:**

How old is the child?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **Between 16 and 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I actually have two children I'm asking about - Alex is 5 years old and Blake is 10, so they're both under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you! So you have two children:
- **Alex** (5 years old)
- **Blake** (10 years old)

Since both children are under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Let me continue with the next step of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about working hours:**

Are both Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?

Given their ages (5 and 10), I'd expect neither of them is in paid employment, but I just need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work at all - they're just kids!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense - at 5 and 10, they're definitely not working. That's a "yes" to the question (they are working less than 24 hours per week). Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about benefits:**

Are Alex and Blake **not** receiving any benefits in their own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit?

Again, given their ages, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm - are neither of them claiming these benefits in their own name?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they don't get any benefits themselves - they're too young for that sort of thing.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly right - children that young wouldn't be receiving those kinds of benefits. So that's a "yes" (they are NOT receiving benefits in their own right). Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about apprenticeships:**

Are Alex and Blake **not** in any apprenticeships?

At ages 5 and 10, I'm confident they aren't, but just to confirm - neither child is enrolled in any kind of apprenticeship programme, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - they're far too young for apprenticeships!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely! Far too young for apprenticeships. That means they're "not in an apprenticeship", so let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check about **your** situation as the person claiming.

**Residency Requirements:**

Do you meet any of the following residency criteria? You only need to meet **one** of these:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - You're a resident in the UK
2. **Settled status** - You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Pre-settled status and working** - You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ continuous months)
4. **Pre-settled status and seeking work** - You have pre-settled status and are actively looking for work
5. **Pre-settled status with sufficient resources** - You have pre-settled status and can financially support yourself/family (and not claiming certain income-based benefits)
6. **Pre-settled status and studying** - You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Pre-settled status as family member** - You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant abroad** - You're living abroad as a Crown servant

Which of these applies to you? For most people, it's simply that they **normally live in the UK**.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, I don't live in the UK at the moment - I'm living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, let me ask a few follow-up questions to see if you might still qualify.

**Do any of these situations apply to you?**

1. **Crown servant living abroad** - Are you (or your spouse/partner) working abroad as a Crown servant? This includes roles like:
   - Diplomatic staff
   - Armed forces personnel stationed overseas
   - Civil servants posted abroad

2. **EU Settlement Scheme** - Do you have either settled status or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme? (Even if you're temporarily abroad, this may still apply)

3. **Temporarily abroad** - Are you normally a UK resident who is just temporarily living abroad?

Could you tell me more about your situation? For example, why are you living abroad and do you have any connection to the UK (like settled/pre-settled status or Crown service)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad - not a Crown servant, don't have any EU settlement status, and I'm not temporarily away. I'm just living outside the UK.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being so clear about your situation. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, I have to deliver some disappointing news.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for UK Child Benefit** for either Alex or Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

### Summary

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| **Alex** | 5 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |
| **Blake** | 10 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |

### Explanation

Both Alex and Blake would qualify for Child Benefit based on their own circumstances - they're under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in apprenticeships. 

However, **you** as the claimant must normally live in the UK (or meet specific exceptions) to receive Child Benefit. Since you're living abroad without settled/pre-settled status, Crown servant status, or other qualifying circumstances, you don't meet the residency requirements.

### What You Can Do

- **If your circumstances change** - If you move back to the UK or gain settled status, you may become eligible at that point
- **Check for other support** - The country you're living in may have its own child benefit or family support programmes
- **Contact HMRC** - If you think your situation might qualify under circumstances we haven't discussed, you can contact HMRC directly for advice: [www.gov.uk/child-benefit](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit)

I'm sorry I couldn't give you better news. Is there anything else I can help you with or clarify?

reasonings: Alex (age 5) meets all child-related eligibility criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements - they are living abroad and do not have settled status, pre-settled status, Crown servant status, or any other qualifying circumstance.

Blake (age 10) meets all child-related eligibility criteria: under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in a disqualifying apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements - they are living abroad and do not have settled status, pre-settled status, Crown servant status, or any other qualifying circumstance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: While both children (Alex, age 5, and Blake, age 10) fully meet the child eligibility requirements for UK Child Benefit, the claimant does not satisfy the residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad without any qualifying circumstances such as settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, Crown servant status, or being temporarily absent from the UK. UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific exceptional circumstances, none of which apply in this case.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
